skill.name: parquet-optimization
skill.description: Analyzes Parquet read and write patterns and recommends targeted improvements for compression, row groups, statistics, projection, and streaming.
skill.summary: Finds practical Parquet performance improvements in data engineering code and design discussions.
skill.author: EmilLindfors

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks as Ruby shell execution and compression guidance as weak cryptography. Review found only documentation and Rust examples, with allowed tools limited to Read, Grep, and Glob. No malicious behavior, network access, secret access, or prompt injection attempt was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged inline Markdown backticks and fenced Rust examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These lines are documentation examples inside SKILL.md, not executable code. The skill header allows only Read, Grep, and Glob, so the cited content does not create command execution risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown inline code or fenced Rust snippets. No executable script file, shell invocation, or user input command construction is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Compression Guidance Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged the description and compression codec guidance as weak cryptographic algorithms. The cited content discusses Parquet compression choices such as ZSTD, LZ4, and Gzip, not cryptographic hashing or encryption. No evidence found of weak cryptography use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are natural language metadata and compression recommendations. They do not implement cryptography or instruct use of weak security algorithms.

content.value_statement: Parquet workloads often become slow because files, row groups, and codecs are poorly tuned. This skill reviews Parquet usage and suggests concrete changes for faster scans, lower storage cost, and safer memory use.
actual_capabilities: Reviews Parquet writer settings for compression, row group size, statistics, and dictionary encoding.
Reviews Parquet reader usage for projection, batch sizing, row group filtering, and streaming.
Explains trade-offs between Snappy, ZSTD, LZ4, and Gzip for common data workloads.
Identifies small-file, partitioning, and cloud object storage retry issues in Parquet workflows.
Provides Rust-focused examples for Arrow and Parquet APIs when relevant.
limitations: It provides guidance only and does not benchmark files directly.
Recommendations depend on workload details such as schema width, query filters, and storage backend.
The examples focus on Rust Arrow and Parquet APIs.
It does not modify files or run performance tests by itself.

use_cases.title: Tune Data Lake Writes
use_cases.description: Review writer settings before publishing large Parquet datasets to object storage.

use_cases.title: Reduce Query Latency
use_cases.description: Find projection, predicate pushdown, and row group improvements for analytics queries.

use_cases.title: Control Memory Usage
use_cases.description: Replace large collects with streaming reads and tuned batch sizes for stable processing.

prompt_templates.title: Review Basic Writer Settings
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a Parquet write path.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Parquet writer setup for compression, row group size, and statistics. Explain the highest impact changes first.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Read Performance
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of scan and read behavior.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this Parquet read path for projection, batch size, row group filtering, and streaming. Suggest changes for lower latency.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Data Lake Layout
prompt_templates.scenario: Planning file sizes and partitions for cloud storage.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help design Parquet file sizing, row groups, partitioning, and compression for a cloud data lake with frequent date filters.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Production Pipeline
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review across writing, storage, and reading.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Parquet pipeline end to end. Prioritize storage cost, scan speed, memory stability, and object store reliability.

output_examples.input: A Rust writer uses default Parquet properties for a large production dataset.
output: The review highlights missing compression, statistics, and row group sizing.
It recommends ZSTD for balanced storage and scan performance.
It explains why explicit writer properties reduce cost and improve pruning.

output_examples.input: A reader loads every column and collects all batches before processing.
output: The review recommends projecting only required columns.
It suggests streaming batches to reduce peak memory use.
It explains when batch size tuning improves stability.

output_examples.input: A dataset contains many small Parquet files in one directory.
output: The review identifies metadata overhead and excess object storage requests.
It recommends batching writes, compaction, and partitioning by common filters.
It describes expected query planning and scan benefits.

best_practices: Share schema details, data size, storage backend, and query patterns with each review.
Benchmark important recommendations before changing production settings.
Prioritize compression, row group sizing, and projection before smaller tuning work.
anti_patterns: Do not apply one compression codec to every workload without checking access patterns.
Do not collect large Parquet datasets into memory when streaming is available.
Do not create many tiny files when downstream engines scan object storage.

faq.question: Does this skill run Parquet benchmarks?
faq.answer: No. It reviews code and designs, then recommends changes to benchmark in your environment.

faq.question: Which languages does it focus on?
faq.answer: The examples focus on Rust with Arrow and Parquet APIs.

faq.question: Can it help with cloud object storage?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers file sizing, row groups, partitioning, and retry concerns for object storage workflows.

faq.question: Will it change my data files?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides analysis and suggestions only.

faq.question: Is ZSTD always the best codec?
faq.answer: No. ZSTD is often a balanced default, but hot data may prefer Snappy for fast decompression.

faq.question: Can it review existing production code?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the read and write paths plus workload details for the most useful review.
